class.name: FmodChannel
class.brief_description: Represents a single playing channel (voice) in FMOD.
class.description: FmodChannel represents an active voice in the FMOD mixer. When a sound is played via [method FmodSystem.play_sound], FMOD allocates a channel to play that sound. This class provides control over playback parameters such as frequency, position, looping, and virtual voice priority. Channels are automatically managed by FMOD's virtual voice system. When there are more sounds playing than physical voices available, lower-priority channels may be "virtualized" (silently processed in the background without consuming hardware resources). [b]Note:[/b] This class extends [FmodChannelControl], which provides additional functionality including volume, pitch, pan, 3D positioning, DSP effects, and more.

method.name: channel_is_null
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the Channel handle is null or invalid. A null channel indicates that the sound has finished playing or the channel has been released.

method.name: channel_is_valid
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the Channel handle is valid and active. A valid channel can be controlled and queried for playback information.

method.name: get_channel_group
method.description: Returns the [FmodChannelGroup] that this channel outputs to. If no channel group has been set, returns [code]null[/code]. The channel group determines the sub-mix that this channel's audio is routed through.

method.name: get_current_sound
method.description: Returns the [FmodSound] currently playing on this channel. Returns [code]null[/code] if no sound is playing or the channel is invalid. This allows you to retrieve information about the playing sound, such as its length or format.

method.name: get_frequency
method.description: Returns the current playback frequency in Hertz (Hz). The default frequency is the sample rate of the sound (typically 44100 or 48000 Hz). Changing the frequency affects both playback speed and pitch.

method.name: get_index
method.description: Returns the index of this channel in the system channel pool. Each channel has a unique index from 0 to the maximum number of channels minus 1. Returns [code]-1[/code] if the channel is invalid.

method.name: get_loop_count
method.description: Returns the number of times the sound will loop before stopping. [b]Values:[/b] - [code]-1[/code]: Infinite looping - [code]0[/code]: Play once (no looping) - [code]1[/code]: Play twice (one loop) - [code]n[/code]: Play n+1 times

method.name: get_loop_points
method.description: Returns a Dictionary containing the loop start and end points. The returned Dictionary has two keys: - [code]"start"[/code]: The loop start position - [code]"end"[/code]: The loop end position The [param timeunit] parameter specifies the unit for these values (milliseconds, PCM samples, etc.). See [enum FmodSystem.FmodTimeUnit] for available time units.

method.name: get_position
method.description: Returns the current playback position. The [param timeunit] parameter specifies the unit for the returned value: - [code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_MS[/code]: Milliseconds (default) - [code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_PCM[/code]: PCM samples - [code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_PCMBYTES[/code]: Bytes of PCM data - [code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_RAWBYTES[/code]: Raw file bytes See [enum FmodSystem.FmodTimeUnit] for all available options.

method.name: get_priority
method.description: Returns the virtual voice priority of this channel. Priority values range from 0 (highest) to 256 (lowest). When FMOD's virtual voice system needs to steal voices, lower priority channels are virtualized first.

method.name: is_virtual
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this channel is being virtualized by the virtual voice system. When virtualized, the channel continues to process silently in the background without consuming a hardware voice. This happens automatically when there are more playing sounds than available physical voices. Use [signal virtualvoice] to be notified when a channel becomes virtual or real.

method.name: set_channel_group
method.description: Sets the [FmodChannelGroup] that this channel outputs to. All audio from this channel will be routed through the specified channel group, allowing for sub-mixing and group-level effects processing. Set to [code]null[/code] to route to the master channel group.

method.name: set_frequency
method.description: Sets the playback frequency in Hertz (Hz). This affects both the playback speed and pitch of the sound. [b]Examples:[/b] - [code]44100[/code]: Normal speed (for 44.1kHz sounds) - [code]22050[/code]: Half speed / one octave lower - [code]88200[/code]: Double speed / one octave higher For pitch shifting without speed change, use the [FmodDSP] pitch shift effect instead.

method.name: set_loop_count
method.description: Sets the number of times to loop before stopping. [b]Values:[/b] - [code]-1[/code]: Loop infinitely - [code]0[/code]: Play once, no looping (default) - [code]1[/code]: Play twice (loop once) - [code]n[/code]: Loop n times (play n+1 times total) The loop points (set via [method set_loop_points]) determine which portion of the sound loops.

method.name: set_loop_points
method.description: Sets the loop start and end points for this channel. When the sound reaches the end point during playback, it will jump back to the start point. The [param timeunit] parameter specifies the unit for the start and end values: - [code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_MS[/code]: Milliseconds (default) - [code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_PCM[/code]: PCM samples - Other time units from [enum FmodSystem.FmodTimeUnit] [b]Note:[/b] You must also set [member loop_count] to enable looping behavior.

method.name: set_position
method.description: Sets the current playback position. Use this to seek to a specific point in the sound. The [param timeunit] parameter specifies the unit for the position value: - [code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_MS[/code]: Milliseconds (default) - [code]FMOD_TIME_UNIT_PCM[/code]: PCM samples - Other time units from [enum FmodSystem.FmodTimeUnit] [b]Note:[/b] Seeking in compressed streams (MP3, Vorbis) may not be sample-accurate.

method.name: set_priority
method.description: Sets the virtual voice priority for this channel. Priority values range from 0 (highest priority) to 256 (lowest priority). When FMOD runs out of physical voices, lower priority channels are virtualized first. Set important sounds (music, dialogue) to high priority (low numbers), and less important sounds (ambient, effects) to lower priority (high numbers).

members.member: The [FmodChannelGroup] this channel outputs to. If not set, outputs to the master channel group. Use channel groups to organize sounds into sub-mixes for group-level control.

Playback frequency in Hertz (Hz). Default is 0, which means the sound's native sample rate. Changing this value affects both playback speed and pitch proportionally. [b]Note:[/b] 0 indicates the default frequency; the actual frequency depends on the sound's sample rate.
Number of times to loop the sound. Default is 0 (play once, no looping). - [code]-1[/code]: Infinite looping - [code]0[/code]: No looping (default) - [code]1+[/code]: Loop n times
Virtual voice priority (0 = highest, 256 = lowest). Default is 0. Lower priority channels are virtualized first when FMOD runs out of physical voices.

signal.name: ended
signal.description: Emitted when playback of the sound has finished. This is triggered when the sound reaches its end and is not looping, or when all loop iterations have completed.

signal.name: occlusion
signal.description: Emitted when 3D occlusion is calculated for this channel. This signal is only emitted when using FMOD's 3D occlusion features. - [param direct_occlusion]: Occlusion factor for direct sound (0.0 = no occlusion, 1.0 = fully occluded) - [param reverb_occlusion]: Occlusion factor for reverberant sound (0.0 = no occlusion, 1.0 = fully occluded) Higher occlusion values typically result in muffled sound (low-pass filtering).

signal.name: syncpoint
signal.description: Emitted when a sync point embedded in the sound file is reached. Sync points are markers that can be embedded in audio files to trigger events at specific times. The [param sync_point_index] parameter indicates which sync point was triggered. Use [method FmodSound.get_sync_point_info] to get information about sync points in a sound.

signal.name: virtualvoice
signal.description: Emitted when this channel becomes virtualized or returns to being a real voice. - [code]true[/code]: The channel is becoming virtual (silently processing without hardware voice) - [code]false[/code]: The channel is becoming real (has been assigned a hardware voice) This happens automatically as part of FMOD's virtual voice system when there are more playing sounds than available physical voices. Channels with lower [member priority] are more likely to become virtualized.
